senators.name: Cory Booker
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Stanford University (Phi Beta Kappa), M.A. from Oxford University as a Rhodes Scholar, and J.D. from Yale Law School. Triple-elite education spanning Stanford, Oxford, and Yale. Career as Newark mayor who applied data-driven approaches to urban governance. Known for meticulous policy work on criminal justice reform, food policy, and housing. Rhodes Scholar credential represents exceptional academic and analytical achievement.

senators.name: Andy Kim
senators.nerdReason: Attended Deep Springs College, then B.A. in political science from University of Chicago (Phi Beta Kappa), and Ph.D. in international relations from Oxford University as a Rhodes Scholar. Doctoral-level academic achievement with elite credentials across three institutions. Career as State Department diplomat in Iraq and Afghanistan, and National Security Council staffer. Applies deep scholarly expertise in international affairs directly to foreign policy. First Korean American U.S. Senator.
education: Ph.D. International Relations, Oxford University (Rhodes Scholar)
B.A. Political Science (Phi Beta Kappa), University of Chicago
experience: U.S. State Department, Afghanistan and Iraq

NJ-01.name: Donald Norcross
NJ-01.notNerdReason: No college degree. Career as union electrician (IBEW) and state legislator. Working-class background with deep labor policy expertise. No academic credentials or evidence-based analytical approach.

NJ-02.name: Jeff Van Drew
NJ-02.notNerdReason: B.S. from Rutgers University and D.D.S. from Fairleigh Dickinson University. Practiced dentistry. Dental degree is health science credential. Switched party from Democrat to Republican in 2019. Dental training shows science education but legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based.

NJ-03.name: Herb Conaway
NJ-03.nerdReason: B.A. from Princeton University, M.D. from Jefferson Medical College, and J.D. from Rutgers Law School. Triple-credentialed with Princeton education, medical degree, and law degree — an exceptionally rare combination. U.S. Air Force Captain (Medical Corps). Career as practicing physician, clinical professor, and attorney. First Black physician with voting privileges in Congress. Applies both medical and legal analytical expertise to healthcare policy.

NJ-04.name: Chris Smith
NJ-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from Trenton State College. Career primarily in advocacy and Congress (serving since 1981). Deep expertise in human trafficking and religious liberty but approach driven by values rather than evidence-based analysis.
experience: Executive Director, New Jersey Right to Life

NJ-05.name: Josh Gottheimer
NJ-05.nerdReason: B.A. from University of Pennsylvania. Career as speechwriter for President Bill Clinton, Microsoft executive, and financial services professional. Known for methodical, bipartisan approach to fiscal policy. Co-chair of the Problem Solvers Caucus. Applies analytical business and financial expertise to tax and economic policy.

NJ-06.name: Frank Pallone
NJ-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from Middlebury College and J.D. from Rutgers Law School. Dean of the New Jersey delegation. Ranking Member of House Energy and Commerce Committee. Decades of deep expertise in healthcare, telecommunications, and environmental policy. Strong policy knowledge but approach is more legislative-institutional than analytically rigorous.
experience: Ranking Member, House Energy and Commerce Committee

NJ-07.name: Thomas Kean Jr.
NJ-07.notNerdReason: B.A. from Dartmouth College and M.A. from the Fletcher School at Tufts University (international relations). Son of former Governor Tom Kean. Dartmouth + Fletcher represents elite analytical training. Career in state legislature. Strong credentials but legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based or research-driven.
education: M.A. International Relations, Fletcher School, Tufts University
experience: New Jersey State Senate Minority Leader

NJ-08.name: Rob Menendez
NJ-08.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill and J.D. from Rutgers Law School. Son of former Senator Bob Menendez. Career as attorney. Legal training provides analytical skills but legislative record too limited to assess evidence-based governance approach.
education: B.A., University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
experience: Commissioner, Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

NJ-09.name: Nellie Pou
NJ-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from Kean University. Career in community organizing and state legislature. New Jersey State Senate Majority Leader. Long political career but no analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
experience: New Jersey State Senate Majority Leader

NJ-10.name: LaMonica McIver
NJ-10.notNerdReason: B.A. from Lincoln University. Career in local government (Newark City Council) and community advocacy. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

NJ-12.name: Bonnie Watson Coleman
NJ-12.notNerdReason: B.A. from Thomas Edison State University. Career in state legislature (first Black woman elected to Congress from New Jersey). Deep legislative expertise in social policy. Experienced but approach not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.
experience: First Black woman elected to Congress from NJ
